text: When working with APIs, you typically send HTTP requests to a server to get or send data. The server often responds with JSON, a lightweight format that is easy to read and parse. Many APIs require authentication, usually with an API key, to ensure only authorized users can access the data. You also need to be aware of rate limits, which control how many requests you can make in a certain time. If you exceed the limit, the API may block you temporarily. Finally, always implement error handling in your code. This helps you manage failed requests or unexpected responses, making your application more reliable.
